payload.text:
when ai generates any code, the response from that code is splitted and not shown. the only response above any code block is shown. fix all the errors. correctly show the code, copy code button and code. ensure: very professional java script / type script and libraries use. fix all the errors and very professional. analyze all the errors and fix. add a button below the input box, add files. alt+a, this lets user to import all the files supported by the gemini api. videos, audios, images, documents and codes. when a file is uploaded, above the input box, add the file name in a

and remove {filename} button below the file name p. add a div role="alert" aria-live="polite" and show alerts like "AI is typing..." "AI replied". etc. now, replace gemini-2.5-flash with gemini-3-flash-preview. system_instructions: "You are an AI assistant developed by stech-vision team. Your goal is to help the users in their everyday tasks. your key capabilities: You can search and browse the web in realtime. You can browse any url in realtime and extract informations from that like from a web page. You can remember the current chat context and continue the context with the user. You can analyze multiple files "Videos, Audios, Images, documents and codes.". You can generate code and build realtime and multi modal apps. You are optimized for chat. You are based on the artificial intelligence. You are trained with a large datasets by stech-vision team. the leader / director: Sujan Rai from Nepal Asia. the startup company in AI and agentic workflow. You are a text processing AI model so you can only work with text and attachments. You can not directly generate visuals "images / videos". Chat with the users in friendly tone and assistant tone. help the users for everything they need.". correctly integrate this system_instruction. search and read the documentations of gemini files api resumable upload and use with gemini api generate content correctly. if a file is uploaded, then when send prompt button is clicked, upload the file to the files api first and use uri with the gemini api. use the same uri in the chat with the chats and api calls until another uri doesn't come. or until another file is not uploaded. add a start a new chat button at the header top ctrl+j which clears everything and starts a new chat. asks for the confirmations to clear and start a new chat. ensure: very professional. add a deep-think toggle button below the add files button which if the toggle button is pressed, add thinking config and "high" in the request body and if not pressed don't add. ensure: very professional. now, build.

**Assessing AI Assistant Update**

I'm currently breaking down the request to update the React AI assistant. My primary goal is to address the issue where code blocks are truncating the following text, and I'm further examining the integration with the Gemini API to ensure a smooth, functional update, including considering any new dependencies.


**Expanding Feature Set**

I've been concentrating on new features, specifically the "Add Files" button for diverse media and documents. I'm building out the file upload handling and designing system instructions to allow user-defined personas. Accessibility is also a priority; I'll implement `role="alert"` for status updates, and I've switched to the latest Gemini model. I am implementing file logic using the Gemini Files API for resumable uploads. I'm incorporating a "New Chat" button and a "Deep Think" toggle, too.


**Revising API Integration**

I'm now zeroing in on the API service to handle the file upload process, particularly because it's frontend only and hitting REST endpoints directly. I'll focus on the resumable upload logic and ensuring `marked.parse` renders code blocks without truncating content. The goal is seamless file handling within the current architecture, addressing the core problem and maintaining the user's expected behavior.


**Developing Upload Functionality**

I'm deep into the implementation plan now. I'm updating `types.ts` to include the required types and moving on to `services/api.ts`, specifically the `uploadFileToGemini` function. I'm focusing on the resumable upload logic and implementing the necessary API calls with polling. Also, I'm updating model name to `gemini-3-flash-preview` and fixing the `MessageBubble.tsx` Markdown rendering to correctly render content.


**Refining Implementation Strategy**

I'm now zeroing in on the UI updates. I'm focusing on incorporating the "Add Files" button and the "Deep Think" toggle, along with a "New Chat" button, and managing the new states. I'm focusing on handling the file uploads when the send button is clicked. Accessibility alerts and system instructions are also on the roadmap. I will also make sure the rendered code blocks are professional looking.


**Focusing Upload Sequence**

I'm now integrating the "send prompt" functionality with the file upload. The plan involves a browser-based upload to the Google AI Studio Files API, paying close attention to header requirements for the process. I'm prioritizing the standard upload flow, which is crucial for the large files the user will be uploading, while keeping in mind the need for resumable upload support.

I'm also working to ensure that the file's URI will be correctly used with the Gemini API.

I'm also re-examining the markdown and code rendering in `MessageBubble.tsx`. Specifically, I'm making sure `marked.parse`'s renderer configuration correctly handles code blocks with appropriate styling, and that the styling doesn't unintentionally hide the content. I'll make sure styling is robust and doesn't affect the correct layout.


**Interpreting "Deep Think"**

I'm now clarifying the implications of "Deep Think," connecting it to the `thinking_config` parameter within the API request. I'm focusing on the interpretation of "high" as a high token limit. This means looking at `thinking_config: { include_thoughts: true }` and examining the specific instruction.


**Implementing "Deep Think"**

My focus is on enabling "Deep Think" functionality. I'm investigating how the model handles thinking and its connection to token limits and instruction interpretation, and clarifying the implementation of the `thinking_config` parameter. I'm also addressing the confusion between a "high" setting and the best way to leverage thinking features, and confirming `thinking_config: { include_thoughts: true }` is the correct approach.


**Optimizing Markdown Rendering**

I'm now zeroing in on robust markdown rendering. I've switched from `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` to a cleaner approach, leaning on a pure and robust `marked` renderer. The aim is to eliminate potential errors from unclosed tags and re-renders, removing the complex logic in the renderer. I'll implement "Copy" functionality with a global listener or a `useEffect` approach. I am modifying these files, `types.ts`, `services/api.ts`, `components/MessageBubble.tsx`, `components/ChatInput.tsx`, and `App.tsx`


**Refining File Upload Logic**

I'm now implementing the resumable upload flow using `fetch`. The process involves initiating the upload with specific headers and metadata, then chunking and sending the file blob to the provided URL. After a successful upload, I'm extracting the `file.uri` from the response, to be stored as `activeFileUri` in the `App.tsx` state. I'm focusing on handling potential errors during the upload and ensuring the URI is correctly integrated. I'm also ensuring that rendering logic can show an entire code block.
Here are the changes to upgrade your application, fix the code rendering issues, implement the file upload workflow with the Gemini Files API, and integrate the new system instructions and controls.

entries.description: Added types for system instructions, file attachments, and reasoning configuration.
Updated to use gemini-3-flash-preview, implemented resumable file upload logic, added system instructions, and deep thinking configuration.
Fixed code block rendering to prevent text splitting, improved styling, and ensured professional display of code and text.
Added file upload support, deep think toggle, accessibility alerts, and refined UI.
Integrated file state management, updated header controls, implemented status alerts, and handled new chat logic.
